text: A Kubernetes Service exposes an application running on one or more Pods through a stable network identity. It helps clients reach a changing set of Pods without hard-coding Pod IP addresses. Common Service types include ClusterIP for internal access, NodePort for exposing a port on each node, and LoadBalancer for cloud-managed external access.

A ConfigMap stores non-confidential configuration data as key-value pairs. Pods can consume ConfigMaps as environment variables, command-line arguments, or mounted files. ConfigMaps are useful when you want to separate configuration from application images.

A Secret stores sensitive data such as tokens, passwords, or keys. Secrets are similar to ConfigMaps in how workloads consume them, but Kubernetes treats Secrets as confidential data and provides extra handling for them. Use a Secret instead of a ConfigMap when the value should not be treated as public configuration.

Ingress manages external HTTP and HTTPS access to Services inside a cluster. It provides routing rules, host-based routing, TLS termination patterns, and a single entry point for multiple Services. Ingress usually works together with an Ingress controller that implements the actual traffic handling.

page_title: Install and Set Up kubectl on macOS
heading_path: Install and Set Up kubectl on macOS
text: On macOS, one common way to install kubectl is through Homebrew. After installation, you can verify the client with 'kubectl version --client'. The Kubernetes docs also describe direct binary installation for users who do not want to use a package manager.

page_title: Run a Stateless Application Using a Deployment
heading_path: Run a Stateless Application Using a Deployment
text: To run a stateless application with a Deployment, create a Deployment manifest or use kubectl create deployment, then inspect the rollout with kubectl get deployments and kubectl rollout status. A Deployment manages ReplicaSets and helps update Pods in a controlled way.

To expose a Service outside the cluster, Kubernetes documentation commonly points to Service types such as NodePort and LoadBalancer, or to Ingress for HTTP and HTTPS traffic. The right choice depends on whether you need raw TCP or UDP exposure, cloud load balancers, or HTTP routing rules.

The Deployment API reference documents fields under Deployment.spec such as replicas, selector, template, strategy, minReadySeconds, paused, revisionHistoryLimit, and progressDeadlineSeconds. These fields control how the Deployment selects Pods and how updates are rolled out.

kubectl apply updates resources by applying a configuration to a resource definition file or stdin. It is commonly used with YAML manifests and works with declarative workflows. The reference page documents flags, examples, and behavior for server-side and client-side apply patterns.
